_comment: Declarative WW FS22->FS25 conversion config (R6). The pipeline (convert.py) reads this; edit here + re-run, never the output. MANDATE (user 2026-07-06): EVERY map-specific value lives here so the tool CORE stays map-agnostic - point it at any FS22 map's config and go. Ship a config per tuned map = 'we support maps X, Y, Z'.

source._CRITICAL: SOURCE OF TRUTH = the FS22 ORIGINAL (read-only). READ the original; GENERATE clean FS25. Never copy map-data.

cfg._note: Core map dimensions shared across tools. map_m = terrain size in metres (WW 16x = 8192).

scene_groups._note: Top-level scene groups to search + subgroup names per class. MAP-SPECIFIC (WW here; West End differs). Empty list [] = let classify.py content-detect instead.

material_remaps._note: WW materials whose textures WW never shipped -> base-game FS25 substitutes (max base-game = upgrade-proof).

assets._note: Chosen base-game FS25 assets referenced per map.

flats._note: build_flats: WW material NAMES to force-treat as co-planar decal overlays (beyond the name-keyword + alphaBlending heuristics). WW-specific.

ground_cover._note: build_ground_cover: base-game FS25 foliage baked over WW's grass pastures (off dirt + fields). fruits VALUES are GE-verified on-disk (NOT a formula - see ww-pasture-foliage memory). pasture_layer/exclude_layers = WW weight-layer names.

tree_palette._note: Base-game FS25 tree species (US) + placement tuning. deciduous = single-file spline rows + lone; conifer = forest stands.

water_plane._note: FS25-NATIVE water (no FS22 assets). ONE oceanShader plane PER connected DEM basin (below capture_level_m), each at default_level_m unless a 'bodies' entry overrides it (nearest component centroid to 'point' [worldX,worldZ]). Planes touching a map edge extend past it (edge_extent_m) for the open-water horizon; the terrain clips each to its waterline. Material = FS25 $data/maps/mapUS/textures/waterplanes.i3d 'waterSim_mat', verbatim. WW: river+west-ocean sit ~1.5 m below the field-41 pond, hence per-basin levels.

farmland_classification._note: Read from the FS22 farmlands.xml per-<farmland> COMMENTS.

ground_layer_map._note: build_ground_texture: WW FS22 weight-layer -> FS25 engine layer (WW's actually-painted subset).

field_entrances._note: build_field_entrances (F1 BUILD): EXECUTES the plan from scan_field_entrances.py (which owns all site selection - deepest-meadow sides, rule out buildings/steep, thread the streetlight gap). For each planned entrance it lays a DUAL wheel-track from the nearest road across the meadow to the field edge (+ into_field_m in). track_layer=mudTracks = the base-game FS25 tire-track terrain texture (what WW field 10 uses via pathway->mudTracks, GT-style); its engine layer mudTracks01 ships wired to the blank weight map, so the tool gives it a dedicated weight PNG (same wiring build_ground_texture uses). two_track=true paints TWO ruts (rut_width_m wide, track_gauge_m apart) with a GRASS CROWN left between them - foliage is cleared ONLY on the ruts (not the whole corridor), so it reads as two wheel tracks, not a bare swath. TREES within clear_trees_m of the entrance mouth OR road_mouth_clear_trees_m of the road crossing OR track_clear_trees_m of ANY point on a rut are removed; a STREETLIGHT is removed only if within clear_lights_m of the crossing or light_lane_clear_m of a rut (poles are pre-threaded).
field_entrances._lock_note: PIN approved entrances so they never move across rebuilds. Path is relative to repo root (survives convert.py's rmtree of out/). Freeze the current plan: `python tools/scan_field_entrances.py --lock`. Thereafter every scan uses locked fields verbatim (no re-derivation, immune to terrain/road/light drift). Unlock a field by deleting its block from the file; re-freeze anytime with --lock. Delete the whole file to go back to fully-auto placement.
field_entrances._scan_note: Scan (site-selection) knobs: sides ranked by meadow DEPTH (how far the meadow apron extends off the plowable edge) - the 2 DEEPEST-meadow sides get the entrances. USER-LOCKED 2026-07-12: it is depth ONLY, not edge length and not area (area wrongly favoured long shallow sides; field 11 belongs on its short 74/84 m-deep ends, not its 26 m-deep long sides). road_reach_m = a chosen side must have a road within this (95 m lets a deep set-back apron still connect); building_clear_m/flat_deg rule out buildings/steep; up to max_per_field spots >= min_entrance_sep_m apart, each threading the streetlight gap. into_field_m kept small: the track only crosses the meadow + tree band to the plowable edge, not into the crop.

productions._note: WW productionPoint placeables -> native FS25 productionPoint on the FS25 GENERIC production i3d ($data/placeables/brandless/productionPointsGeneric/<name>) which ships input trigger + pallet-output spawn + POI. Its VISUAL is HIDDEN (renderable shapes -> nonRenderable, occluder off) so WW's OWN extracted building is what you see; FS22 <productions>+<storage> are injected for faithful input/output cycles, and triggers are snapped to the FS22 point's OWN authored node positions. Drops the base-game placeable (kills the visual double-up). The FS25 generic is auto-picked by OUTPUT fill-type overlap (works map-agnostically - WW raisinFactory auto-maps to grapeProcessingUnit by its GRAPEJUICE+RAISINS outputs), so 'map' below is only an OVERRIDE for points auto-match gets wrong / has no analog. out_subdir holds the generated XMLs + hidden i3ds.

map._note: OPTIONAL override: WW placeable basename -> FS25 generic folder. Empty = fully auto (output-overlap match).

trigger_offset._note: Optional [dx,dz] fine-tune NUDGE on top of the FS22-aligned triggers. build_placeables rewrites the hidden generic i3d's trigger nodes to the FS22 point's OWN authored locals (unload on the apron, WRENCH at the door), so at the raw WW origin they already match WW's building - add an entry only if a specific point needs a small manual shift.

crops._note: build_crops: WW custom FS22 crops -> FS25. Auto-classifies each WW fruitType: BASE (already in the terrain fruits-FML) skipped; PORT if WW ships maps/foliage/<crop>/ (converted FS22->FS25 foliageType); NATIVE if FS25 ships $data/foliage/<crop>/ (e.g. onion - just referenced). categories = fillTypeCategories each raw crop joins (combine harvest + trailer haul + sell); windrow_categories = for crop windrow fills (bales).
crops._harvest_note: fruitTypeCategories = which HARVESTER/planter a crop works with (FS25 GRAINHEADER=combine grain header, DIRECTCUTTER=draper, SOWINGMACHINE=seeder can plant it, MOWER, etc). The map's <fruitTypeCategories> REPLACES the base list, so build_crops emits the FULL base membership + these additions. Beet/root harvesters (TOPLIFTINGHARVESTER) can't take custom fruits without a custom header -> cabbage/onion get NO harvest group for now (plantable, not harvestable).
crops._plant_note: plant_categories are applied to EVERY custom crop (incl. cabbage/onion) so any sower/planter can plant them: SOWINGMACHINE=seeder, PLANTER/PLANTER_SMALL=precision planters.

placeable_map._note: build_placeables (NON-selling placeables only; selling stations are handled by 'sellpoints'). override = WW placeable basename -> FS25 base-game placeable path (under $data/placeables/). skip = WW-custom placeables with no FS25 analog. Everything else auto-matches by name against the FS25 placeables index.

buypoints._note: FS22 buyingStation placeables (BUY bulk goods into a trailer) -> native FS25 buy points (buyingStation+loadTrigger) on the sell-platform model at the exact FS22 spot. These are dropped by 'sellpoints' (that path is for sellingStations). Each 'convert' entry: name = price-screen label; fills = explicit buyable list (used when the FS22 source can't be read, e.g. base-game gasStation); add = extra fill types appended (LIME/SEEDS/DEF). Unknown-to-FS25 fills are filtered; added fills the game lacks are ignored, so DEF works only if a DEF mod is present.

sellpoints._note: WW selling stations (type=sellingStation) are GENERATED as native FS25-schema station XMLs at maps/<out_subdir>/, referenced $mapdir$/maps/<out_subdir>/<name> (the shipping-FS25 Kansas pattern). Each carries WW's storeData <name> (correct price-screen label - fixes 'Harbor doesn't show') + broad fillTypeCategories (accepts EVERY FS25 crop/product, DLC-proof - fixes 'crops can't be sold') on the base-game sellingStationGeneric i3d (a VISIBLE sell platform - the FS22 sell points dynamically loaded this same generic model; a trigger-only base left the sell points with no distinctive shape). The placeable is offset-compensated (unload-node local offset + WW net-yaw) so the unload trigger lands on the exact FS22 spot. Production points are NOT here.

name_overrides._note: WW names that are FS22 base-game $l10n_* keys (FS25's key namespace differs -> would show raw key). Bake clean literals.

tweaks._note: build_tweaks (FINAL step): per-map manual OVERRIDES applied to the generated output as the last word, so one-off corrections survive every re-run without hand-editing (convert.py clobbers the output). Each entry calls out ONE thing by name and nudges it. where=placeable (maps/placeables.xml, match by storeData name/filename/uniqueId) or i3d (map scene graph, match by node name or parent>child>leaf). action=rotate|translate|scale|delete. rotate yaw/pitch/roll + translate dx/dy/dz + scale are DELTAS/multipliers on the existing value. Matches fail-loud on 0 or >1 hits.

list._comment: sell point faces the wrong way in FS25; rotate 90deg.

#3: the us_cross road-surface tile (shapeId 489, 'us_cross_..._tmpl') is COPLANAR with its sibling shapeId 490 at all ~248 crossings -> z-fight shimmer + edge seam. Nudge every 489 tile local Z +2mm to break the coplanarity (verified in GE on one instance). No area box = applies to every us_cross map-wide.

autodrive._note: build_autodrive.py (separate from convert.py - a savegame add-on): generates a TWO-LANE, US right-side AutoDrive network by skeletonizing the painted road surface (road_layers) into centerlines, then emitting two one-way lanes offset lane_offset_m to the RIGHT of each travel direction, junction-stitched, DEM-heighted, into savegame<savegame>/AutoDrive_config.xml. Load the save FRESH after (AutoDrive reads on load, rewrites on save). right_hand_sign flips which side the lanes fall on (Giants +Z=south handedness -> -1 = drive-on-right; flip to +1 if they come out on the wrong side).
